title: Boot, Partition, Install, Upgrade, Migration, and Recovery · Mastery Check
summary: A support panel compares near-miss cases for boot, partition, install, upgrade, migration, and recovery and accepts only safe, authorized, causal, accessible, privacy-preserving, and independently defensible decisions. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for reversible operating-system deployment runbook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inventory compatibility, support, data, encryption keys, applications, accounts, drivers, firmware, licenses, network, power, access needs, backup, and rollback before change.
Trace firmware mode, Secure Boot, device order, boot manager, loader, recovery environment, signed media, architecture, and trusted source.
Plan partition tables, system and recovery partitions, volumes, filesystems, labels, mount points, encryption, capacity, and data-preserving boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Postdeploy Verify
steps.instruction:
quiz case — An encrypted fleet deployment changes firmware, boot media, partitions, drivers, apps, profiles, access settings, licenses, and rollback at once. The quiz variation 4 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Verify boot, activation, updates, drivers, identity, encryption, applications, user data, accessibility, security baseline, backups, recovery, and changed-device behavior.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Postdeploy Verify.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for verify boot, activation, updates, drivers, identity, encryption, applications, user data, accessibility, security baseline, backups, recovery, and changed-device behavior. For CompTIA A+ support Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Postdeploy Verify, keep Postdeploy Verify connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

stimulus.title: Postdeploy Verify failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Reaching the desktop proves an operating-system deployment is complete.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Verify boot, activation, updates, drivers, identity, encryption, applications, user data, accessibility, security baseline, backups, recovery, and changed-device behavior.

options.text: For diagnose the hidden failure, apply one familiar repair, omit user impact, authorization, safety, asset and version identity, baseline, recent change, competing cause, prediction, observation, rollback, changed-case verification, accessibility, privacy, escalation, and controlled transfer, then accept a missing symptom as support proof.
Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.
Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority. That claim fails the evidence boundary for Postdeploy Verify.

For CompTIA A+ support Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Postdeploy Verify, keep Postdeploy Verify connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Verify boot, activation, updates, drivers, identity, encryption, applications, user data, accessibility, security baseline, backups, recovery, and changed-device behavior.

hints: For CompTIA A+ support Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Postdeploy Verify, name the user impact, fictional ticket, asset, environment, authorization, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, prevention, escalation, accessible documentation, and transfer boundary, Postdeploy Verify precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At CompTIA A+ support Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Postdeploy Verify, trace this concrete input and predict user impact, safety and authority, baseline, reproduction, timeline, hypothesis, bounded test, observation, rejection, repair, rollback, original and changed-case verification, prevention, escalation, and controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: An encrypted fleet deployment changes firmware, boot media, partitions, drivers, apps, profiles, access settings, licenses, and rollback at once. The quiz variation 6 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Verify boot, activation, updates, drivers, identity, encryption, applications, user data, accessibility, security baseline, backups, recovery, and changed-device behavior.

For CompTIA A+ support Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Postdeploy Verify, challenge the documented misconception with changed-user, device, version, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, access need, hypothesis, test result, repair, restart, recurrence, escalation, and controlled equipment-transfer evidence: Reaching the desktop proves an operating-system deployment is complete.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.
